DRAHER FOR EVIDENTIARY HEARING AND APPROVAL OF CREDITOR'S CLAIM AND FOR PREFERENCE., CALENDAR OF OCT-23-2008 THE TENTATIVE STANDS: AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR APPROVAL OF CREDITOR'S CLAIM - TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED. 10 DAYS LEAVE FOR MR. CONRAD TO FILE AN OBJECTION. AS TO THE MOTION FOR PREFERENCE - THE CONTINUED DATE WAS CHANGED FROM 11/10/08 TO NOVMEBER 17, 2008 AT 9:00 AM BY WHICH POINT THE CREDITOR'S CLAIM SHOULD BE AT ISSUE. ASSUMING THAT THE CREDITOR'S CLAIM IS AT ISSUE BY 11/17/08, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS THAT THE MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED. FURTHER ASSUMING THAT THE CREDITOR'S CLAIM IS AT ISSUE BY 11/17/08 AND THE MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED, THE COURT WILL SET THE MATTER FOR TRIAL AND THEREFORE APPEARANCES ARE REQUIRED.

calendar.matters:
Ex Parte Application For Distribution Of Funds Held In Attorney Trust Account For Heirs Of Wilhelm Dombrower, Declaration Of Daniel A. Conrad In Support Of Ex Parte Application
Off Calendar Aug-19-2011 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-23-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Petition Of Personal Representative For (I) Approval Of Settlement Agreement And (Ii) Creditors Claim Of Personal Representative
Off Calendar Dec-02-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Shirley Feldman. Cynthia Gilman for Gloria Draher (Administrator). Dennis Livingston for Gloria Draher (Individually).

Mr. Conrad stated that the remaining issue was the one-seventh to Wilhelm, that they have filed both Wilhelm's will and his Wife's will, that Wilhelm did not leave probate assets, that Stephen will be appointed Executor of Margot's Estate, that he will be entitled to those assets, and that there should not be a hold up as to the intestate heirs. In addition, Mr. Conrad suggested that the Court grant the petition, suggested that Wilhelm's one-seventh be held in his Trust account, and that he would then come back and petition since Stephen is Executor. Ms. Gilman stated that her client is eighty-five, is in poor health, and that they support Mr. Conrad's suggestions. Mr. Livingston stated he also supported Mr. Conrad's suggestions. Mr. Conrad stated that everyone with an interest in the one-seventh has signed a consent to the settlement. The petitions were granted (subject to review of the written orders). All parties submitted proposed orders in open court.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition Of Personal Representative For (I) Approval Of Settlement Agreement And (Ii) Creditors Claim Of Personal Representative
Off Calendar Oct-01-2009 Continued To Oct-20-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Livingston for Gloria Draher individually. Daniel Conrad for Shirley Feldman. Cynthia Gilman for Gloria Draher (Executor).

Mr. Conrad stated that the only remaining issue was the 1/7 distribution which is to go to the Wilhelm Queen Company surrogates, stated that the Decedent left everything to Margot Draher, requested that the Court grant the petition and stated that the settlement agreement called for the 1/7 distribution to be held in his client/trust account. Ms. Gilman stated that the Estate did not object to Mr. Conrad's proposal. Mr. Livingston stated that there was no objection to what Mr. Conrad was proposing. The matters were taken under submission. All parties are to submit proposed orders. Status on the issue regarding the remaining 1/7 distribution was set for November 24, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition Of G. Draher For Evidentiary Hearing And Approval Of Creditor'S Claim And For Preference And Points And Authorities In Support Thereof.
Off Calendar Dec-23-2008 Text Ruling

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Preference
Off Calendar Dec-05-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Shirley Feldman. Dennis Livingston for Gloria Draher (Claimant). Cindy Gilman for Gloria Draher (Administrator).

The Court stated that the Creditor's Claim was now at issue and that the tentative on the Motion For Preference was to grant the motion. The Parties stated that there were no objections to the tentative. Mr. Livingston will submit the order for granting of the Motion For Preference. The Parties informed the Court that discovery still needed to be conducted, stated that mediation could be a possibility and requested the matter be set for trial. Upon signing of the order granting the Motion for Preference, the Court will also sign the order referring the matter to the department of the Presiding Judge to set the matter for trial.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer By Shirley R. Feldman To Petitiion Of Gloria M. Draher For Evidentiary Hearing And Approval Of Creditor'S Claim And For Preference.
Off Calendar Oct-23-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Shirley Feldman. Dennis Livingston for Gloria Draher (Claimant).

Mr. Conrad stated that he submitted to the tentative on the demurrer, that he had 3 new clients, Relatives that had been referred to him and that he would be filing a request for special notice on their behalf as they had not been served. Mr. Livingston stated that he also went with the tentative. The Court informed Counsel that notice was to be sent to the Franchise Tax Board and to Ms. Gilman.

The tentative stands: As to the cause of action for Approval of Creditor's Claim - Tentative ruling: Overruled. 10 days leave for Mr. Conrad to file an objection.

As to the motion for preference - the continued date was changed from 11/10/08 to Novmeber 17, 2008 at 9:00 am by which point the creditor's claim should be at issue. Assuming that the creditor's claim is at issue by 11/17/08, the tentative ruling is that the motion for preference is granted. Further assuming that the creditor's claim is at issue by 11/17/08 and the motion for preference is granted, the Court will set the matter for trial and therefore appearances are required.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Peppina Thompson, CSR#7433. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer By Shirley R. Feldman To Petitiion Of Gloria M. Draher For Evidentiary Hearing And Approval Of Creditor'S Claim And For Preference.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Livingston for Gloria Draher (Petitioner). Daniel Conrad for Shirley Feldman. Cynthia Gilman for the Administrator.

Mr. Livingston reported that they had commenced discovery, that there was discussion regarding mediation and that both sides needed to complete discovery. Mr. Conrad agreed with Mr. Livingston. The matter was continued to December 23, 2008 for further status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-19-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Cynthia Gilman for Gloria Draher (Petitioner). Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Daniel Conrad for Shirley Feldman (hEIR).

Mr. Ferdon stated that he received a copy of the petition, that he waived notice and that the SFPA does not have priority due to an heir coming forward and being represented by Counsel. In addition, Mr. Ferdon stated the possibility of maternal heirs that were known at this time. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court). Bond is set at $250.000.00.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Joann Prior CSR 129. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.
